sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper

train.py implements the sigmoid gate g_t = σ(β·Δ_t) on the detached Teacher-Student log-probability gap Δ_t = log π_teacher(y_t|·) − log π_student(y_t|·), with stop-gradient applied to the gate so gradients flow only through the student log-probability, as described in Section 2.3 and Appendix A (Propositions 1–2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The OPSD auxiliary loss L_OPSD is computed as a masked token-level average over student-sampled tokens using the single-sample reverse-KL surrogate −log π_student(y_t|·) weighted by the gate g_t, and added to the GRPO loss as L = L_GRPO + λ·L_OPSD with λ=0.1, exactly as specified in Section 2.2/2.3 and Table 3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Three gating strategies are implemented and selectable: Entropy gate σ(β·H_t), Gap gate σ(β·Δ_t), and Soft-OR gate σ(β·H_t)+σ(β·Δ_t)−σ(β·H_t)·σ(β·Δ_t), with β=10 as the default sharpness, matching Section 2.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The GRPO baseline is implemented with the clipped surrogate using group-relative advantages computed from sequence-level environment rewards over G=8 rollouts per prompt (Section 3.1, Appendix Algorithm 2 and Table 3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: All five baselines — GRPO, OPSD, Skill-SD, GRPO+OPSD, RLSD — are re-implemented per Appendix Algorithms 2–6, including RLSD's advantage re-weighting by the self-teacher gap and Skill-SD's importance-weighted f-divergence distillation with KM-retrieved skills as privileged context.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Four skill-retrieval strategies are implemented over the SkillRL SkillBank: UCB Retrieval (Eq. 1 with running-mean reward μ_s, total queries N, selection count n_s, and exploration constant c), Keyword Matching, Full Retrieval, and Random Retrieval, as described in Section 2.1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity

ALFWorld training uses the GiGPO (Feng et al. 2025) data split with 16 tasks per batch, 8 rollouts per prompt, and maximum prompt length 2,048 tokens, and evaluation spans the six task categories Pick, Look, Clean, Heat, Cool, and Pick2 (Section 3).

Search-QA training follows the Search-R1 setup using E5 (Wang et al. 2022) as the retriever, with NQ and HotpotQA as in-domain training data and TriviaQA, PopQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue, and Bamboogle as out-of-domain evaluation; batch size is 128 tasks with max prompt length 4,096 tokens (Section 3).

WebShop training uses 1,000 selected training tasks with 16 tasks per batch, 8 rollouts per prompt, and max prompt length 4,096 tokens; evaluation uses the same 128 fixed validation tasks as Feng et al. 2025 (Section 3).

Skill context for training is sourced from the SkillRL SkillBank (Xia et al. 2026) across all three environments, and the prompt templates (Figures 15–17) populate {skill_context} only during training while leaving it empty at inference time (Section 3, Appendix C).
Experiment execution and reproducibility

Training is executed for 150 steps on 8×H800 GPUs using the Qwen2.5-Instruct and Qwen3-Instruct base models (specifically Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, and Qwen3-1.7B) as stated in Section 3.

All three model scales (Qwen2.5-3B, Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3-1.7B) are trained on each of the three environments (ALFWorld, WebShop, Search-QA), producing nine SDAR runs that match the experimental matrix of Table 1 and Figures 10–14.

Method-specific hyperparameters from Table 3 are applied per baseline, including λ=0.1 distillation coefficient, β=10 sigmoid sharpness, PPO clip ratio ε, KL penalty α_KL toward the reference policy, group size G=8, and KM as the default skill-retrieval strategy.

Training logs/checkpoints record the five diagnostic curves of Figures 10–14 (Gate Active Ratio, Gate Mean, OPSD Loss, Teacher-Student Gap, Reward) for each (model, environment) pair across the 150 training steps.
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness

Inference evaluation of SDAR is performed WITHOUT skill context (empty {skill_context} in the prompts of Figures 15–17), while Skill-GRPO* and Skill-Prompt evaluations include retrieved skills at inference, as described in Section 3.1.

Robustness analysis (Table 2) sweeps the four retrieval strategies — UCB, KM, Full, Random — with the fixed optimal configuration (β=10, λ=0.1) and reports ALFWorld, WebShop-Score, and WebShop-Acc compared against the w/o OPSD GRPO baseline (Section 3.3).

Ablations report (a) gate-type comparison (Entropy vs Gap vs Soft-OR) on Qwen2.5-3B (Figure 6/7), (b) β sweep including β=0 (no gating) on Qwen2.5-3B (Figure 7), (c) λ sweep including λ=1.0 over-weighting and λ near 0 (Figure 8), and (d) divergence comparison (reverse KL vs forward KL vs JSD) on Qwen2.5-7B (Figure 9, Section 3.4).
Result match versus the paper's reported targets
On Qwen2.5-3B, SDAR improves over GRPO by approximately +9.4% on ALFWorld (84.4 vs 75.0), +7.0% on Search-QA, and +4.7% on WebShop-Acc, as reported in Table 1 and the abstract.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On Qwen2.5-7B, SDAR delivers the abstract's headline gains over GRPO: +9.4% on ALFWorld, +7.0% on Search-QA, and +10.2% on WebShop-Acc (Section 1 abstract and Table 1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On Qwen3-1.7B ALFWorld, SDAR reaches the highest score of approximately 53.9%, beating GRPO (46.1), Skill-GRPO (21.1), RLSD (42.2), and the GRPO+OPSD collapse case (32.0), matching Section 3.2 / Table 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Standalone OPSD collapses on Search-QA to near zero and GRPO+OPSD degrades catastrophically on Qwen3-1.7B (≈32.0 vs GRPO's 46.1), while SDAR remains stable across all (model, environment) cells of Table 1 (Section 3.2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Robust-retrieval Table 2 results reproduce: Random Retrieval still beats the w/o-OPSD GRPO baseline on all three of ALFWorld, WebShop-Score, and WebShop-Acc; KM is competitive with or surpasses UCB on WebShop (Section 3.3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance

The reproduction code is organized in line with the public release at https://github.com/ZJU-REAL/SDAR (referenced in the abstract), with separate runnable scripts/configs for SDAR and each of the five baselines (GRPO, OPSD, Skill-SD, GRPO+OPSD, RLSD).

Final results are emitted as a Table-1-style artifact reporting success/accuracy/score per (method, model, environment) cell for Qwen2.5-3B, Qwen2.5-7B, and Qwen3-1.7B across ALFWorld, Search-QA, and WebShop.

Training-dynamics plots replicating Figures 10–14 (Gate Active Ratio, Gate Mean, OPSD Loss, Teacher-Student Gap, Reward Curve) are produced for each (model, environment) combination and saved as exported figures or logged tensors.
The full prompt templates of Figures 15–17 for ALFWorld, Search-QA, and WebShop, with the {skill_context} slot, are committed in the repository alongside the SkillRL SkillBank used at training time.
